finding: handleAtSelect (prompt-input.tsx:590-599) is NOT updated. When a user selects a delegate type option, option.type === 'agent' is false, so it falls through to `addPart({ type: 'file', path: option.path, ... })`. Since {type:'delegate'} has no `path` property, this will either cause a TypeScript compilation error or produce a ContentPart with undefined.path. The plan incorrectly claims 'No changes to createPill/parseFromDOM/addPart code', but handleAtSelect is the bridge between popover selection and addPart — it MUST handle the new 'delegate' variant.
repair_path: Add a third branch to handleAtSelect: `if (option.type === 'delegate') { addPart({ type: 'agent', name: option.name, content: '@' + option.name, start: 0, end: 0 }) }` — delegates still create type:'agent' ContentParts for pipeline compatibility.

finding: groupBy (prompt-input.tsx:625-628) is NOT updated. Delegates with `type:'delegate'` won't match `item.type === 'agent'` and won't have `item.recent`, so they'll be grouped as `'file'`. This causes subagent delegates to appear under the file section in the @mention popover, confusing users.
repair_path: Add groupBy case: `if (item.type === 'delegate') return 'delegate'` and add 'delegate' to the rank sort function (rank 0.5 or adjacent to agent).

finding: Plan claim: 'No changes to ContentPart, AgentPart, createPill, parseFromDOM, addPart, buildRequestParts, or the submit pipeline.' This is true for those specific functions, but omits handleAtSelect which IS the pipeline entry point that creates the ContentPart. The 'no changes' list is misleading — handleAtSelect must be changed.
repair_path: Update the 'No changes to' section to accurately state: handleAtSelect needs a new branch (but creates the same AgentPart type, so downstream remains unchanged).

finding: The plan specifies only manual verification steps (bun typecheck + manual click-through). Zero automated tests for the @mention system despite it having 4+ moving parts (agentList memo, useFilteredList filter/groupBy, handleAtSelect, atKey, rendering branch in slash-popover). A regression would go undetected until a developer manually types @ in the composer.
repair_path: Add at minimum a component-level test: render PromptPopover with atFlat containing a delegate item, verify icon difference, verify atKey produces distinct key. Or add a unit test for the agentList → atKey → handleAtSelect flow with mock data.

finding: filterKeys in useFilteredList (prompt-input.tsx:624) only specifies ['display']. For delegate items, display is `agent.name`, but description is not searchable. Users searching by a subagent's description text won't find it. Not a blocker, but a UX gap.
repair_path: Add 'description' to filterKeys if delegate items carry descriptions that users should be able to search by.

finding: Objection 1: handleAtSelect not updated. `option.type === 'agent'` is false for delegate. Falls to else branch accessing `option.path` which doesn't exist on delegate type. Either TS error or runtime broken pill.
repair_path: Add handleAtSelect branch for type:'delegate' that creates {type:'agent', name, content} ContentPart — delegates reuse agent pipeline.

finding: Objection 2: groupBy not updated. Delegate items fall through to `return 'file'` group — subagents appear under file section instead of their own visual group.
repair_path: Add `if (item.type === 'delegate') return 'delegate'` to groupBy. Add 'delegate' to rank sort with rank 0.5 (between agent 0 and recent 1).

finding: F1 resolved: handleAtSelect now includes option.type === 'delegate' in the agent branch condition. Delegates route to addPart({ type: 'agent', name: option.name }) instead of crashing on option.path. Both type narrowing and runtime behavior are correct.
repair_path: Already applied in Fix E. No further action needed.

finding: F2 resolved: groupBy now checks item.type === 'delegate' first, returning 'delegate' group. sortGroupsBy assigns rank 0.5 to delegate, placing it between agents (0) and recent files (1). Subagents no longer fall through to the file group.
repair_path: Already applied in Fix F. No further action needed.

finding: Group headers require structural changes to PromptPopover — the current flat `For each={props.atFlat.slice(0, 10)}` in slash-popover.tsx:70 cannot render section labels between groups. Two options: (1) change atFlat from a flat list to a grouped data structure passed to PromptPopover, or (2) compute group boundaries from the flat list at render time. Option 2 is simpler but means the `slice(0, 10)` must happen AFTER grouping, not before, or a group header might appear without items. The plan must specify which approach and verify the slice/group interaction.
repair_path: Replace the flat `For` loop with grouped rendering that maps group categories → section header → items. Change `slice(0, 10)` to slice within each group or slice after group flattening. Move the grouping logic from `useFilteredList` back to the render path, or compute group transitions in PromptPopover by comparing adjacent item keys.

finding: Visual distinction between agent and delegate pills (FI-054) is not addressed. `createPill` at prompt-input.tsx:702 sets `data-type="agent"` for ALL agent-type parts, regardless of whether they originated from a delegate selection. Both the DOM attribute and the visual rendering are identical. `handleAtSelect` at prompt-input.tsx:594 converts both agent and delegate selections into `{type: "agent", name: ...}` — the delegate origin is lost at insertion time. This means `pushAgent` at prompt-input.tsx:832 has no way to distinguish delegates from agents when parsing the DOM for submission.
repair_path: Either (a) carry delegate origin through the pipeline: add `data-subagent` to the pill DOM, pass `{type: "delegate"}` through AgentPart (requires type expansion), and read it back in pushAgent, or (b) accept that delegate pills look identical to agent pills in-editor and defer visual distinction as a conscious design gap. If option (b), document this decision in the plan.

finding: Rank collision between delegate and agent groups (FI-023). Both categories use rank 0 in `sortGroupsBy` at prompt-input.tsx:637-641. The ordering between delegates and agents depends on insertion order (delegates first in agentList construction), not on deterministic comparison. When names sort across groups, items interleave non-deterministically. The prefab mentions ordering but does not prescribe a fix for this collision.
repair_path: Either (a) merge delegate and agent into a single "people" group (same rank, same group key) so they sort by name together, or (b) assign delegate rank 0.5 (between agent at 0 and recent at 1) so delegates sort after agents but before recent files. Option (a) is simpler and matches user expectation of a unified "select an agent" experience. Option (b) is better if the UX distinction between direct agents and subagent delegates is important.

finding: Frecency/usage tracking (Scope item #4) adds a new persistence concern without a corresponding follow-up issue. None of FI-022, FI-023, FI-053, FI-054, FI-055 require frecency. This feature would add: (a) a local storage key for usage counts, (b) an increment call on delegate select, (c) a sort override in agentList/useFilteredList. The storage key would need cleanup on revert. This is not a "CSS/component change only" item — it has data persistence implications. If frecency is required, the plan should define the storage key, cleanup strategy, and confirm it doesn't conflict with the existing file-recent tracking.
repair_path: Either (a) defer frecency to a follow-up issue and explicitly mark it as out of scope for this lane, or (b) specify the storage mechanism (Persist utility already imported), data model, and revert/cleanup plan.

finding: Keyboard navigation (Scope item #5) — the existing `handleKeyDown` at prompt-input.tsx:1139 delegates popover arrow keys to `atOnKeyDown` and `slashOnKeyDown` from `useFilteredList`. The cursor position is maintained correctly because `event.preventDefault()` is called before the popover key handler runs. No conflict with history navigation (ArrowUp/ArrowDown checks `collapsed` cursor state). The plan's keyboard nav concern appears to be already handled by `useFilteredList`'s internal active-item cycling.
repair_path: Verify that `useFilteredList`'s `atOnKeyDown` handles the new delegate entries without changes. If the hook already works for AtOption union types, keyboard nav for delegates is already functional.

finding: Two filter pipelines (composer dropdown vs @mention popover) use different exclusion criteria. The prefab correctly flags this but defers unification. The architect should document whether this lane: (a) acknowledges the divergence and defers unification, (b) partially unifies the filter logic into a shared helper, or (c) pins a follow-up issue to unify post-lane. Without a decision, the divergence silently widens when the plan adds new group headers only to the @mention side.
repair_path: Add a note to the plan explicitly stating the unification decision. If deferred, file a follow-up issue linking to this plan. If unifying in this lane, extract `isAgentFiltered(name, mode)` into a shared utility imported by both surfaces.

finding: Delegate descriptions lost on commit (FI-053). `handleAtSelect` at prompt-input.tsx:594 converts delegate selection to `{type: "agent", name: option.name}` — the delegate's `description` field is silently dropped. The AgentPart type (not shown in read, but referenced as `AgentPart` in createPill at line 702) has no description field. This means the delegate description shown in the popover is permanently gone once committed.
repair_path: Either (a) add an optional `description` to AgentPart type, store it in a `data-description` attribute on the pill, and pass it through the pipeline, or (b) explicitly document that delegate descriptions are popover-only and not preserved in-editor. Option (b) is adequate for v1 if the plan acknowledges it.

finding: @general subagent support (Scope item #3) — verifying the data model: agentList (prompt-input.tsx:1310+) constructs delegate-type AtOptions from agents where `agent.mode === 'subagent'`. This is purely client-side: it reads from the agents query result which already includes mode. No server-side changes needed. The `buildRequestParts` pipeline handles agent selection from the submitted prompt. The claim that this is client-side only is correct.
repair_path: No action needed. Verify by confirming `agent.mode` exists in the SDK agent type and subagent-mode agents are correctly filtered by checking `agentsQuery.data` for mode field.
